test_cases.title: Enable a single notification channel independently
preconditions: All notification channels (email, in-app, push notification) are currently disabled
steps: Navigate to the notification preferences settings
Enable the email channel only
Save or confirm the preference change
Trigger a new notification event
test_cases.expected_result: The notification is delivered via email only; in-app and push notification channels do not receive the notification.

test_cases.title: Disable one channel without affecting other enabled channels
preconditions: All three notification channels (email, in-app, push notification) are currently enabled
steps: Navigate to the notification preferences settings
Disable the email channel
Save or confirm the preference change
Trigger a new notification event
test_cases.expected_result: The notification is delivered via in-app and push notification channels; no notification is sent via email.

test_cases.title: Changes to channel preferences take effect immediately for new notifications
preconditions: All notification channels are currently enabled
steps: Navigate to the notification preferences settings
Disable the push notification channel
Save or confirm the preference change without restarting the session or application
Trigger a new notification event immediately after saving
test_cases.expected_result: The new notification is delivered via email and in-app channels only; no push notification is sent, with no delay or session restart required.

test_cases.title: Disable all notification channels simultaneously
preconditions: All three notification channels are currently enabled
steps: Navigate to the notification preferences settings
Disable the email channel
Disable the in-app channel
Disable the push notification channel
Save or confirm the preference changes
Trigger a new notification event
test_cases.expected_result: No notification is delivered through any channel (email, in-app, or push notification).

test_cases.title: Unauthenticated user cannot change notification channel preferences
preconditions: No user is currently authenticated (user is logged out)
steps: Attempt to directly access the notification preferences settings page or API endpoint
Try to toggle or submit a change to any notification channel preference
test_cases.expected_result: The system denies access to the notification preferences settings and does not apply any channel preference changes for the unauthenticated request.

test_cases.title: Each channel can be toggled independently across all three channels
preconditions: All three notification channels are currently disabled
steps: Enable the in-app channel only and save
Trigger a new notification event; verify only in-app notification is received
Enable the push notification channel in addition and save
Trigger a new notification event; verify in-app and push notifications are received
Enable the email channel in addition and save
Trigger a new notification event; verify all three channels deliver the notification
test_cases.expected_result: At each step, only the currently enabled channels deliver notifications, confirming each channel is controlled independently.

assumptions: A 'save' or 'confirm' action may or may not be required after toggling; tests assume a save step exists but note this may be implicit (auto-save).
A 'notification event' can be reliably triggered in the test environment to observe delivery behavior.
Each delivery channel's output (email received, in-app badge/message visible, push notification received) is observable and verifiable during testing.
User preferences are stored per-user account, not per-device or per-session.
The system exposes some form of notification preferences UI or API endpoint that allows toggling individual channels.
notes: The requirement does not specify the UI mechanism (toggle, checkbox, etc.) for enabling/disabling channels, so test steps are kept generic. It is not stated whether disabling a channel affects in-flight or queued notifications, only 'new notifications' — tests are scoped accordingly. There is no mention of a default state (all enabled, all disabled) when a user account is first created; this is a coverage gap. The requirement does not define what constitutes 'immediately' in measurable terms (e.g., within N seconds), making it difficult to set a precise boundary for the immediacy test. No mention of rate-limiting or concurrent preference change conflicts is made.
